steps.title: Identify the common standard
steps.content: Both texts evaluate how well experimental designs exclude nutrient pathways other than CMN. Text 1 emphasizes that Xiao used barriers to separate roots—a crucial step Wahbi did not take. Text 2 then exposes why even barriers fail: they must be permeable to hyphae, which means they must also allow liquids.

steps.title: Apply the standard to each answer
steps.content: Both authors would answer “No”—Wahbi's study did not effectively exclude other pathways. Text 1 directly faults Wahbi for omitting barriers; Text 2's logic (barriers permit liquids, leaving alternative routes open) strengthens that verdict for a design without barriers. Questions A, C, and D disrupt agreement: A asks whether Xiao's barriers actually work (the authors disagree), C asserts a biological mechanism neither text establishes, and D concerns ideal barrier materials (only Text 2 addresses). This matches choice B — B is correct.
